popularStory.headline: Louis IV (German: Ludwig; 1 April 1282-11 October 1347)
popularStory.description: Louis IV (German: Ludwig; 1 April 1282-11 October 1347), called the Bavarian, of the house of Wittelsbach, was King of the Romans from 1314, King of Italy from 1327, and Holy Roman Emperor from 1328. …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Ludwig IV von Bayern於1282年4月1日出生在德國貝也藍德可萊斯 明陳時，他的父親Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rhein52歲，母親Mathilde Gräfin von Habsburg29歲。他於1308年10月14日與Beatrix von Silesia在德國貝也明陳結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1347年10月11日在德國貝也藍德可萊斯 費爾斯騰費爾德布盧可費爾斯騰費爾德布盧可普享年65歲，埋葬在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ludwig IV von Bayern于1282年4月1日出生在德国貝也藍德可萊斯 明陳时，他的父亲Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rhein52岁，母亲Mathilde Gräfin von Habsburg29岁。他于1308年10月14日与Beatrix von Silesia在德国貝也明陳结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1347年10月11日在德国貝也藍德可萊斯 費爾斯騰費爾德布盧可費爾斯騰費爾德布盧可普享年65岁，埋葬在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Bayern
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German (and possibly also Dutch)   Bayer   or   Beyers  , or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname. Compare   Byers  .

variants.name: Bayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Kurt, Otto, Klaus, Manfred, Bernd, Bernhard, Erwin, Franz, Hans, Johann, Juergen.

German, Scandinavian, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Bavaria (German Bayern). This region of southern Germany derives its name from that of the Celtic tribe of the Boii who once inhabited this area. They were displaced in the 6th century AD by an ancient Germanic people, the Boioarii or Baiuarii, whose name is derived from that of their Celtic predecessors. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Czechia, Slovakia, and Poland. Compare Beyer and Payer .

English (Lincolnshire): occupational name for a maker of baize cloth, from an agent derivative of Old French baies, Middle English bayes (from the adjective bai ‘reddish-brown, bay’), probably so called because of its original color. This material was said to have been introduced into Britain by immigrants from France and the Netherlands in the 16th century, but the word certainly appears much earlier in English. The surname may also be topographic denoting a ‘dweller by the bend’, from an agent derivative of Old English bēag ‘bend’.

nameMeanings.name: Ludwig
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from hlud ‘fame’ + wīg ‘war’; an ancient royal and imperial name. See Louis .

variants.name: Louis
meanings: name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hlōd ‘fame’ + wīg ‘war’. It was very common in French royal and noble families. Louis I ( 778–840 ) was the son of Charlemagne, who ruled as both King of France and Holy Roman Emperor. Altogether, the name was borne by sixteen kings of France up to the French Revolution, in which Louis XVI perished. Louis XIV, ‘the Sun King’ ( 1638–1715 ), reigned for seventy-two years ( 1643–1715 ), presiding in the middle part of his reign over a period of unparalleled French power and prosperity. In modern times Louis is also found in the English-speaking world (usually pronounced ‘loo-ee’). In Britain the Anglicized form Lewis is rather more common, whereas in America the reverse is true.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Ludwig IV von Bayern was born on 1 April 1282, in München, Bavaria, Germany, his father, Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rhein, was 52 and his mother, Mathilde Gräfin von Habsburg, was 29. He married Beatrix von Silesia on 14 October 1308, in Munich, Bavaria, Germany. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 11 October 1347, in Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Fürstenfeldbruck, Bavaria, Germany, at the age of 65, and was buried in Holy Roman Empire.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ludwig IV von Bayern est né le 1 avril 1282, à Landkreis München, Bayern, Allemagne, son père, Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rhein, avait 52 ans et sa mère, Mathilde Gräfin von Habsburg, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Beatrix von Silesia le 14 octobre 1308, à München, Bayern, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 11 octobre 1347, à Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Landkreis Fürstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Allemagne, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Saint-Empire romain.

de.Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Bavaria, Germany: Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Landkreis Fürstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Deutschland
de.lifeSummary: Als Ludwig IV von Bayern am 1. April 1282 in Landkreis München, Bayern, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rhein 52 und seine Mutter Mathilde Gräfin von Habsburg 29. Er hat am 14. Oktober 1308 in München, Bayern, Deutschland Beatrix von Silesia geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 11. Oktober 1347 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Landkreis Fürstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Deutschland und wurde in Heiliges Römisches Reich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ludwig IV von Bayern è nato il il 1 aprile 1282 nel Landkreis München, Bayern, Germania, il suo padre, Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rhein, aveva 52 anni e il suo madre, Mathilde Gräfin von Habsburg, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Beatrix von Silesia il 14 ottobre 1308, nel München, Bayern, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 11 ottobre 1347, nel Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Landkreis Fürstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Germania, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolto a Heiliges Römisches Reich.

ja.lifeSummary: Ludwig IV von Bayernが1282年4月1日にドイツバイエルンランドクレイス ムuンチェンで生まれた時，父Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rheinは52歳，母Mathilde Gräfin von Habsburgは29歳であった。1308年10月14日にドイツバイエルンムuンチェンでBeatrix von Silesiaと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1347年10月11日にドイツバイエルンランドクレイス フuルステンフエルドブルックフuルステンフエルドブルックプウチで65歳で死去し，ヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチにて埋葬された。

ko.Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Bavaria, Germany: 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 프u르스펠드브럭 프u르스펠드브럭 푸치
ko.lifeSummary: Ludwig IV von Bayern(이)가 1282년 4월 1일 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 u천에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rhein(은)는 52세였고, 그의 어머니 Mathilde Gräfin von Habsburg(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1308년 10월 14일 독일 베이른 u천에서 Beatrix von Silesia(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1347년 10월 11일 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 프u르스펠드브럭 프u르스펠드브럭 푸치에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치에 묻혔다.

pt.Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Bavaria, Germany: Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Landkreis Fürstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Alemanha
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ludwig IV von Bayern nasceu em 1 de abril de 1282, em Landkreis München, Bayern, Alemanha, seu pai, Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rhein, tinha 52 anos e sua mãe, Mathilde Gräfin von Habsburg, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Beatrix von Silesia em 14 de outubro de 1308, em München, Bayern, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 11 de outubro de 1347, em Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Landkreis Fürstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Alemanha, com 65 anos, e foi sepultado em Heiliges Römisches Reich.

ru.Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Bavaria, Germany: пуч, фuрстенфелдбрукк, ландкрис фuрстенфелдбрукк, беиирн, Германия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ludwig IV von Bayern родился 1 апреля 1282 г. в местности ландкрис мuнчэн, беиирн, Германия, его отцу по имени Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rhein было 52 года, а матери, которую звали Mathilde Gräfin von Habsburg, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Beatrix von Silesia 14 октября 1308 г. в местности мuнчэн, беиирн, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 11 октября 1347 г. в местности пуч, фuрстенфелдбрукк, ландкрис фuрстенфелдбрукк, беиирн, Германия в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности хилигесь рoмищесь раич.

es.Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Bavaria, Germany: Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Landkreis Fürstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Alemania
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ludwig IV von Bayern nació el 1 de abril de 1282, en Landkreis München, Bayern, Alemania, su papá, Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rhein, tenía 52 años y su mamá, Mathilde Gräfin von Habsburg, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Beatrix von Silesia el 14 de octubre de 1308, en München, Bayern, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 11 de octubre de 1347, en Puch, Fürstenfeldbruck, Landkreis Fürstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Alemania, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultado en Heiliges Römisches Reich.
